title: cerastium decalvans f. leontopodium
intro: Cerastium decalvans f. leontopodium, also known as Snow in Summer, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the carnation family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and is known for its small, white flowers and silvery-gray foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems, due to its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cerastium decalvans f. leontopodium include relief from colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory infections, as well as support for the immune system, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves.
